title: Jump Game II
problem_statement: Return the minimum number of jumps needed to reach the last index of the array, where each element indicates the maximum jump length from that position.

examples.explanation: Jump from index 0 to 1, then from 1 to 4.

constraints: It is guaranteed that you can reach the last index.
intuition: Greedily choose the farthest reachable position within the current range and take a jump when that range ends.

approaches.name: Greedy range expansion
approaches.description: Track the current end of the jump range and the farthest reachable next position, then increment jumps when the current range is exhausted.
approaches.why_not: DP is possible but less efficient because greedy directly yields the minimum number of jumps for this reachable array.

interview_qa.q: Why does the greedy range method work?
interview_qa.a: Because taking the farthest reachable step within each jump range minimizes the total number of jumps.

interview_qa.q: What triggers a jump count increase?
interview_qa.a: When the current traversal index reaches the end of the current jump range.
